cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. The question is asking why Doyle was surprised. The story goes that he's getting into a taxi and the driver immediately addresses him by name, asking where to take him. Doyle is surprised and asks if the driver has seen him before, to which the driver says no. Then the driver explains how he figured out it was Doyle based on clues like the newspaper story, his skin color, ink spot on fingers, and his clothes. The key point here is that the driver knew his name and correctly identified him without having met him before. The options are A to D. Let's look at the choices again. A) the driver had seen him before. But the driver said he hadn't, so that's not it. So A is out. B) the driver could call him by his name. Yes, that's exactly what happened. The driver called him "Mr. Doyle" right away. That's why Doyle was surprised, because the driver knew his name without being told. So B seems correct. C) the driver was waiting for him. The story says the driver turned to him after he got into the taxi. There's no mention of the driver waiting specifically for him, just that it's a taxi stop where people returning from Marseilles come. So C might not be right. D) the driver answered no questions from him. Wait, the driver did answer questions. Doyle asked if the driver had seen him before, and the driver replied. So D is incorrect.
